none: Central Valley project, San Felipe Division : environmental impact statement.

The San Felipe Division of the Central Valley Project aims to provide supplemental water to Santa Clara and San Benito Counties in California. This project involves constructing facilities to import water from the Central Valley through a tunnel and distribute it via conduits and pumping plants. The goal is to stabilize groundwater levels, prevent land subsidence, and support urban growth. However, it will impact local wildlife habitats and archaeological sites. The project is expected to enhance recreational use by stabilizing water levels and maintaining fishery pools.
Central Valley project, San Felipe Division : environmental impact statement.

The San Felipe Division of the Central Valley Project aims to provide supplemental water to Santa Clara and San Benito Counties in California. This project involves constructing facilities to import water from the Central Valley through a tunnel and distribute it via conduits and pumping plants. The goal is to stabilize groundwater levels, prevent land subsidence, and support urban growth. However, it will impact local wildlife habitats and archaeological sites. The project is expected to enhance recreational use by stabilizing water levels and maintaining fishery pools.
Santa Clara and San Benito Counties, California
Sierra Club, Northern California Regional Conservation Committee

The San Felipe Division was proposed to address water supply issues in rapidly growing areas of Santa Clara and San Benito Counties, where groundwater levels were declining and land subsidence was becoming a concern. While there was some public pushback regarding the potential environmental impacts, particularly on wildlife habitats and archaeological sites, the project was ultimately seen as necessary for supporting urban development and recreational needs. The project has been implemented to help manage water resources more effectively in the region.
